Welcome to the post office! How can I help you?
Hi! I have a postcard for my mom. I want to send it to her in Brazil.
That is abroad, so you need a stamp for one euro eighty. We send it by air mail.
Perfect. I also have two postcards for my grandparents. Can I send them too?
Of course. Did you write the address on each one? And do you have a parcel too?
Yes, the addresses are ready! I have a small parcel for my dad. Can you weigh it and give it to him?
I want to send it to her in Brazil.
Can I send them too?
How much is a stamp for Brazil?
Can you weigh this parcel?
Where is the mailbox?

Hello, and welcome to the post office! How can I help you today? If you want to send a postcard, it is very easy. First, write the message and the address on the back. Then, choose a stamp. For a postcard inside France, the stamp is one euro twenty. To send it abroad, for example to Brazil, the stamp is one euro eighty, and we send it by air mail. If you have a letter, put it in an envelope first, and write the address clearly. For a parcel, I weigh it here on the scale, and the price depends on the weight. When everything is ready, you can put your postcard in the yellow mailbox outside, or give it to me and I will send it for you. Is there anything else I can do for you?

Today I went to the post office in Paris to send postcards to my family. I wrote a short message on each one and copied the address carefully. For my best friend, I wrote a postcard and I sent it to her by air mail. For my grandparents, I bought two postcards and I sent them to Brazil too. The clerk helped me choose the stamps and he weighed a small parcel for my mom. I put the postcards in the yellow mailbox outside. It feels so special to write to people you love when you are far away. I cannot wait for my family to read the messages and see the beautiful pictures of Paris!

First, you buy a postcard and you write a short message on the back. Next, you copy the address of your family carefully. After that, you go to the post office and you choose a stamp. Then, you give the postcard to the clerk and you pay. Finally, you put it in the yellow mailbox and you send it to them.
